description_long: This is a small, rustic stone house with a single story and a pitched, clay-tiled roof that shows signs of wear and partial collapse. The exterior walls are constructed from irregularly shaped, light-colored stones, giving it a traditional, rural appearance. A single window with a wooden frame and flowerpots is visible on one side, while a blue and red planter sits near the wall on a concrete base. The roof is partially covered with green foliage, suggesting some overgrowth or proximity to vegetation. The rear and one side of the house are less detailed, with the roof and wall textures becoming more simplified and less defined. The overall structure appears aged and weathered, with a utilitarian and modest design typical of rural or countryside dwellings.
description: A small, rustic stone house with a worn clay-tiled roof, single window, and visible planter, exuding a traditional countryside charm.
description_view_0: The main side view shows a stone wall, a wooden-framed window with flowerpots, and a blue-red planter on a concrete base.
description_view_1: A narrow side view dominated by the sloped roof and some visible foliage, with the planter partially visible at the edge.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the large, plain expanse of the roof and a mostly featureless stone wall, with minimal architectural detail.
description_view_3: The gable end view displays the full height of the tiled roof, a stone wall, and a section of the concrete ground.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple entryway with a single door, no porch, and minimal decorative elements
